body:
Official 2019 Supporter Survey

We need to stay focused and energized as we enter 2019 and
welcome a new Democratic majority in the House. Let us know what
your priorities are →
( https://act.myngp.com/forms/-1362245504026015232?fn=%7B%7BFirstName,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&ln=%7B%7BLastName,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&add1=%7B%7BAddressLine1,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&add2=%7B%7BAddressLine2,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&em=%7B%7BRecipientEmailAddress,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&pc=%7B%7BZipCode,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&ci=%7B%7BCity,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&st=%7B%7BState,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&hp=%7B%7BPhoneNumber,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&oc=%7B%7BOccupation,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&ep=%7B%7BEmployer,DefaultTo=%7D%7D )

In less than a month a new session of Congress will begin,
with Democrats as the majority in the House. There’s a lot on the
agenda, and Mike wants to hear from you. What issues do you want
Democrats to address first?
( https://act.myngp.com/forms/-1362245504026015232?fn=%7B%7BFirstName,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&ln=%7B%7BLastName,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&add1=%7B%7BAddressLine1,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&add2=%7B%7BAddressLine2,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&em=%7B%7BRecipientEmailAddress,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&pc=%7B%7BZipCode,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&ci=%7B%7BCity,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&st=%7B%7BState,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&hp=%7B%7BPhoneNumber,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&oc=%7B%7BOccupation,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&ep=%7B%7BEmployer,DefaultTo=%7D%7D )

name: Mary Mancini
subject: Join us Monday, Dec. 10 in Cookeville!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY, DR
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/-vQ4rHEPxkzGyfqVbSx7FQ50uBGsqMhQ2iTUXCVPqkM=/5iZlka-saIoIix7oRvZCIRcNSZ5qB5zLZboizqNEnhY=)
Dear Peter,
On Monday, December 10, we're heading to Putnam County for the last stop on our statewide **_ANALYZE, ORGANIZE, MOBILIZE_** Tour! Join us from **6pm-8pm CT in Cookeville** for a public forum -- an opportunity for you, as a member of the Tennessee Democratic Party, to discuss the 2018 election cycle and our path forward in 2019, 2020, and beyond.

**It 's free to attend, but please [click here to RSVP](https://act.myngp.com/el/-vQ4rHEPxkzGyfqVbSx7FQ50uBGsqMhQ2iTUXCVPqkM=/uZ1k2OGcWm1Sr65h5VI87_g7D9ruqT_U1LY0tcdqIRM=) to let us know you're coming. **

Opportunities to [**sponsor the tour**](https://act.myngp.com/el/-vQ4rHEPxkzGyfqVbSx7FQ50uBGsqMhQ2iTUXCVPqkM=/Z1g9Xojil9MlQSGimYq62tybTUYk81Yn2_Um7ie9fLY=) are also available to help defray costs. Sponsorship levels are detailed below.

And **[share the event on Facebook](https://act.myngp.com/el/-vQ4rHEPxkzGyfqVbSx7FQ50uBGsqMhQ2iTUXCVPqkM=/7aYGw5ximLBw8d8By7pJPmCdF59LT5YnLicJ0sIBqYM=)** to spread the word and invite friends, family, and neighbors.

Thank you so much for your support. We look forward to seeing you!

Mary and the TNDP Team
\--
Mary Mancini
Chair, Tennessee Democratic Party

* * *

[****](https://act.myngp.com/el/-vQ4rHEPxkzGyfqVbSx7FQ50uBGsqMhQ2iTUXCVPqkM=/uZ1k2OGcWm1Sr65h5VI87_g7D9ruqT_U1LY0tcdqIRM=)
**TNDP Analyze **• **Organize **• **Mobilize Tour**
Monday, Dec. 10, 2018 | 6pm-8pm CT
Putnam County Democratic Party Headquarters
[111 N. Cedar Ave, Cookeville, TN 38501](https://act.myngp.com/el/-vQ4rHEPxkzGyfqVbSx7FQ50uBGsqMhQ2iTUXCVPqkM=/nbkPJluKylQfv5u5V6YCMjMCXKbENzVSgICFWrbDdtI=)

Join the TNDP and our sponsors:

**Sponsorships available:**

Click [**here**](https://act.myngp.com/el/-vQ4rHEPxkzGyfqVbSx7FQ50uBGsqMhQ2iTUXCVPqkM=/uZ1k2OGcWm1Sr65h5VI87_g7D9ruqT_U1LY0tcdqIRM=) to RSVP. Purchase sponsorships [**here**](https://act.myngp.com/el/-vQ4rHEPxkzGyfqVbSx7FQ50uBGsqMhQ2iTUXCVPqkM=/Z1g9Xojil9MlQSGimYq62tybTUYk81Yn2_Um7ie9fLY=) or contact Sandra Sepulveda at [sandra@tndp.org](https://act.myngp.com/el/-vQ4rHEPxkzGyfqVbSx7FQ50uBGsqMhQ2iTUXCVPqkM=/heGFxOBcK12M9sQO2OCLCO8rYVR0Jf80Zq_rGzPetG0=) or 615-327-9779.

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: The First Family Meets Privately with George W., Laura Bush to Offer Condolences
body:
"President Trump and first lady Melania Trump met privately Tuesday evening with former President George W. Bush and former first lady Laura Bush..." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=79b03fbe58&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **The First Family Meets Privately with George W., Laura Bush to Offer Condolences**

“President Trump and first lady Melania Trump met privately Tuesday evening with former President George W. Bush and former first lady Laura Bush on the eve of funeral services for former President George H.W. Bush,” Brett Samuels reports in _The Hill_. Thousands of Americans have visited the Capitol this week to pay respects to former President Bush, whose remains lay in state there until this morning.

President Trump has declared today a national day of mourning, coinciding with State Funeral services for our 41st President at the National Cathedral.

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=dad5bce9d1&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| In _[The Hill](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3432df5a9a&e=3ee4985eea)_ , Emily Birnbaum reports that First Lady Melania Trump hosted members of the Bush family, including former First Lady Laura Bush, for a special tour of the White House Christmas decorations. “A sweet visit during this somber week," the former First Lady wrote on Instagram. “Thanks to Mrs. Trump for inviting our family to the White House to see the Christmas decorations and our old friends, the residence staff.”
---
|
---
| Today’s State Funeral for former President George H. W. Bush “will be attended by President Trump and the four living ex-presidents—Jimmy Carter, Bill Clinton, George W. Bush and Barack Obama,” David Sim of _[Newsweek](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1efa6e4372&e=3ee4985eea)_ reports. “Dignitaries from around the world are expected to attend the state funeral, including Britain’s Prince Charles and former Prime Minister John Major, German Chancellor Angela Merkel, King Abdullah II and Queen Rania of Jordan, and Poland's President Andrzej Duda and former President Lech Walesa.”

**More** : [President Trump’s Proclamation on the death of George H. W. Bush](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4ee193a1db&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5e460bbd2e&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4407f97a22&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=bb6872f610)
---
| | | The White House * 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW * Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111
---

name: NHGOP Press & Communications
subject: #FITN = Defended
body:
**STATEMENT BY WAYNE MACDONALD ON SECRETARY OF STATE VOTE** **For Immediate Release: **December 5, 2018 **Contact: **Joe Sweeney603-225-9341[Joe@nhgop.org](mailto:joe@nhgop.org) **Concord, NH** – New Hampshire Republican Party Chairman Wayne MacDonald released the following statement regarding today's vote for Secretary of State: “Today, on Organization Day, New Hampshire Democrats suffered their first major legislative defeat, despite having an outright majority in both chambers of the General Court. It is good news that a handful of Democrats joined our Republican members in re-electing Bill Gardner to the position of Secretary of State. The Democrat Caucus tried all it could, including organizing an unprecedented caucus vote for Secretary of State, but they simply couldn’t overcome a unified Republican Party and their own members bucking their majorities to maintain integrity in our elections and our First-in-the-Nation Primary. Maybe now Colin Van Ostern will finally realize that the people of New Hampshire don’t really want him in a leadership position.” ###
---
---
| This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail.com](mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com)

clean_body: STATEMENT BY WAYNE MACDONALD ON SECRETARY OF STATE VOTE For Immediate Release: December 5, 2018 Contact: Joe Sweeney603-225-9341mailto:joe@nhgop.org Concord, NH – New Hampshire Republican Party Chairman Wayne MacDonald released the following statement regarding today's vote for Secretary of State: “Today, on Organization Day, New Hampshire Democrats suffered their first major legislative defeat, despite having an outright majority in both chambers of the General Court. It is good news that a handful of Democrats joined our Republican members in re-electing Bill Gardner to the position of Secretary of State. The Democrat Caucus tried all it could, including organizing an unprecedented caucus vote for Secretary of State, but they simply couldn’t overcome a unified Republican Party and their own members bucking their majorities to maintain integrity in our elections and our First-in-the-Nation Primary. Maybe now Colin Van Ostern will finally realize that the people of New Hampshire don’t really want him in a leadership position.” ### | This email was sent to mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com Republican Party of New Hampshire 10 Water Street Concord New Hampshire 03301

name: Team NHGOP
subject: THIS WEEKEND: Volunteer Appreciation Events!
body:
Derek, We want to thank you for all your hard work this past election cycle in securing victory! There are a few volunteer appreciation events coming up **this weekend** , and we'll have more to come. These events include complimentary hors d'oeuvres to kick off the holiday season!

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: In photos: America’s solemn farewell to its 41st President
body:
This morning at 11 a.m. ET, President Donald J. Trump, First Lady Melania Trump, and each of America’s four living past Presidents... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cc4e4a670&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • December 5, 2018
---
| | |
# **In photos: America’s solemn farewell to its 41st President**

**This morning at 11 a.m. ET** , President Donald J. Trump, First Lady Melania Trump, and each of America’s four living past Presidents and their spouses attended the State Funeral honoring the life of President George H. W. Bush.

**“He looked for the good** in each person, and he usually found it," President George W. Bush said of his father during his eulogy at the National Cathedral in Washington. “Dad taught us that public service is noble and necessary, that one can serve with integrity and hold true to the important values like faith and family.”

**President Trump** , the First Lady, Vice President Mike Pence, and Second Lady Karen Pence all visited the U.S. Capitol earlier this week as President Bush lay in state. “When President George Herbert Walker Bush left office, he left America and the world more peaceful, prosperous, and secure,” Vice President Pence said on Monday.

**President George W. Bush** concluded his eulogy with a message directly for his father. “We're going to miss you,” America’s 43rd President said to its 41st. “Your decency, sincerity, and kind soul will stay with us forever.”

**Indeed, it will.**

[_In photos: America’s solemn farewell to its 41st President_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2933cc5b43&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|

| **The funeral procession of former President George H. W. Bush passes in front of the White House en route to the Washington National Cathedral** | December 5, 2018
